title: Silky Spicy Dutch Pork Stir-Fry
description: This recipe creates a nostalgic pork dish infused with spicy flavors and silky texture, perfect for diabetic-friendly dining.
instructions: In a bowl, mix soy sauce and cornstarch until smooth. Add ginger paste and stir evenly.
Slice the pork tenderloin thinly and marinate in the mixed ingredients for at least 30 minutes.
Heat oil in a wok or large frying pan over medium heat. Sear the marinaded pork on all sides, about 5-7 minutes per side until golden brown.
Add bell peppers and green onions to the pan; stir-fry for another minute. Pour the soy sauce mixture into the pan.
Increase heat to high and let it simmer for about 2 minutes or until thickened. Serve hot with steamed rice.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
